text: In machine learning, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) is a supervised algorithm used for classification and regression. In a hard-margin SVM, the mathematical objective is to strictly maximize the geometric margin between classes, which is achieved by minimizing the squared norm of the weight vector.

Decision Tree regressors recursively partition the feature space into distinct regions to predict continuous values. To prevent severe overfitting on the training data, techniques such as cost-complexity pruning are applied to collapse leaf nodes that do not significantly decrease the overall variance.

A Fenwick Tree, or Binary Indexed Tree (BIT), is a data structure in C++ used for calculating prefix sums and updating elements in an array. It achieves both operations in logarithmic time complexity by cleverly utilizing the binary representation of array indices to traverse the tree structure.

Disjoint Set Union (DSU) is a highly efficient graph data structure used to track a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets. By implementing both path compression and union by rank, DSU guarantees nearly constant time complexity for its find and merge operations.

In power system engineering, the Newton-Raphson method is a powerful iterative mathematical technique used for load flow analysis. It relies heavily on calculating a Jacobian matrix to determine the voltage magnitudes and phase angles at various buses, converging much faster than simpler algorithms.

The Gauss-Seidel method is an alternative iterative technique for solving power flow equations. While it is easier to program in software like MATLAB and requires less memory per iteration than Newton-Raphson, it scales poorly for massive grids due to its strictly linear convergence rate.

In control systems engineering, a Lead Compensator is primarily designed to improve the transient response of an unstable or sluggish system. By adding a phase lead to the open-loop transfer function, it effectively increases the phase margin and overall dynamic stability.

The Nyquist stability criterion is a graphical technique utilized in control theory to determine the stability of a closed-loop system. By mapping the open-loop transfer function's frequency response onto the complex plane, engineers can easily count encirclements of the critical point at negative one.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a modern AI architecture that connects large language models to external, proprietary databases. Instead of relying solely on the model's internal training weights, a RAG pipeline retrieves factual text chunks and injects them into the final prompt to eliminate hallucinations.

Okapi BM25 is a sparse retrieval function used natively by traditional search engines to estimate lexical relevance. It heavily relies on Inverse Document Frequency (IDF) to assign massive mathematical weight to rare keywords while penalizing common words like conjunctions and prepositions.
